clauses.plain_summary: The treaty attaches at USD 5,000,000: the reinsurer's liability applies only to the part of each loss occurrence above this retention. The cedent keeps losses at or below USD 5,000,000.
The per-occurrence limit is USD 10,000,000 — the most the reinsurer pays for any single loss occurrence (written as '10,000,000 xs 5,000,000').
One reinstatement is available at 100% additional premium, pro rata as to amount: after a loss erodes the limit, cover is restored once, giving an annual aggregate limit of USD 20,000,000.
War, terrorism, and nuclear perils are excluded from the layer; a loss arising from these causes does not attach to the treaty or erode the limit.
The cedent must advise the reinsurer of any loss occurrence reaching 50% of the retention (USD 2,500,000) within 30 days, and provide a loss bordereau quarterly.

data_gaps: O-3 (Hailstorm, Region A) falls at or below the USD 5,000,000 attachment — illustrated at zero layer recovery.
narrative: Treaty TRTY-9001 is a 2026 excess-of-loss cover for Cedent Mutual Insurance Co., incepting 2026-01-01 and expiring 2026-12-31, in USD. The layer is 10,000,000 excess of 5,000,000: the reinsurer's liability applies to the part of each loss occurrence above the USD 5,000,000 retention, up to USD 10,000,000 per occurrence. One reinstatement at 100% additional premium (pro rata as to amount) restores the limit once, giving an annual aggregate of USD 20,000,000. War, terrorism, and nuclear perils are excluded. The cedent must notify occurrences reaching 50% of the retention within 30 days and file a quarterly bordereau. As an illustration under these terms, applying the reported occurrences in order: O-1 (gross 12,000,000) produces a ceded recovery of 7,000,000; O-2 (gross 18,000,000) produces 10,000,000 and reinstates 7,000,000 of limit for a reinstatement premium of 1,400,000; O-3 (gross 3,000,000) is below the attachment and produces zero. Illustrative total ceded recovery is USD 17,000,000, leaving USD 3,000,000 of the aggregate. These figures are illustrative arithmetic under the layer terms and do not determine actual recoverability for any occurrence. Informational interpretation only; not a coverage or recoverability determination, reserving or accounting decision, or legal advice.
